title: MC80a Star Cruiser

The '''MC80a Star Cruiser''', also styled as '''MC80A Star Cruiser''', was a [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]]-focused variant of the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80]] [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] fielded by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and subsequently the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

==Characteristics==
The MC80A Star Cruiser was a variant of the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80]] [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] produced by [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari shipyards]] following their [[Calamarian Revolt|liberation]] from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] occupation in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. Unlike earlier MC80 variants, converted from [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|exploration vessels]] and [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|luxury liners]] into [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the MC80A was designed as a [[Warship/Legends|warship]], leaner<ref name="EGW" /> and more powerful than its predecessors.<ref name="EGVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

[[File:Unidentified_Mon_Calamari_vessel_Endor.png|thumb|left|200px|An MC80a Star Cruiser among the Rebel fleet massing near Sullust]]

The MC80a cruiser was a 1,200 [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] with a 5,402-person crew, and had an additional [[Trooper/Legends|troop]] complement of 1,200. The [[Starship/Legends|vessels']] 48 [[Taim & Bak/Legends|Taim & Bak]] [[XV9 turbolaser]] batteries were guided by a [[Plat Dromma]] [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]], and their three [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] [[Hangar/Legends|bays]] carried six starfighter [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]: three of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]], one of [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]], and two of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]]. The MC80a was equipped with a [[Serridge/Legends|Serridge]] [[SEAL Shielding System/Legends|SEAL shielding system]] and [[TriLuna]] [[400MGS stardrive and hyperdrive system]], reaching a 60 [[megalight]] top sublight speed<ref name="Screen" /> via ten clustered [[Engine/Legends|engine thrusters]] at the ventral stern of their rounded, oblong, [[Color/Legends|gray-colored]] exterior, which was dotted with pods and bulges, and featured a single elevated pod located midship on certain examples of the craft,<ref name="Episode VI" /> and further stern on others.<ref name="CTD" /> Its scalloped [[Hull/Legends|hull]]<ref name="DW">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> had an organic, nature-mimicking appearance resembling the [[Ray/Legends|ray]],<ref name="FF12">{{FactFile|12|HOM 1-2|''Home One'' (MC80 Mon Calamari Star Cruiser)}}</ref> a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Fish/Legends|fish]].<ref name="Ray">{{CSWECite|III|83-84|Ray}}</ref>

==History==
By the time of the buildup to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> this starship was incorporated into the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of the Rebel Alliance and was active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Rescue of Han Solo/Legends|rescue]] of newly-minted Rebel [[General/Legends|general]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Alliance starships converged near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], while personnel gathered on the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']] to establish the plan of attack against the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].<ref name="Episode VI" />

[[File:MC80a-Star-Cruiser-Executor-view.png|thumb|right|300px|MC80a Star Cruisers at the Battle of Endor]]
One of these vessels was in formation near ''Home One'' prior to the fleet's [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump to Endor. At least ten MC80A cruisers participated in the attack, which was in reality an Imperial trap and erupted into a battle resulting in an Alliance victory, with at least six of the cruisers surviving the engagement.<ref name="Episode VI" />

The MC80A remained in use following the transformation of the Rebel Alliance into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. One such cruiser, the ''[[Naritus]]'', was dispatched to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] on anti-[[Pirate/Legends|piracy]] missions, during which the ship's [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Calamarian]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Genkal]], was personally responsible for putting down the pirate band the [[Khuiumin Survivors]].<ref name="CTD" />

[[File:Naritus.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The New Republic MC80a cruiser ''Naritus'' led the evacuation of Thanta Zilbra]]
During the [[Corellian Crisis]] of [[18 ABY/Legends|18 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the ''Naritus'', now considered "aging" by retired New Republic [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Kursk Mal'ia]], was among the Republic's few active capital ships.<ref name="CTD" /> While assigned to patrol duty in the [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]], the ''Naritus'' used a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] to apprehend an unidentified [[X-TIE Fighter]]. The starfighter's [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], undercover [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] agent [[Belindi Kalenda]], was detained aboard the cruiser before being turned over to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], to whom she revealed a plot to to trigger a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Thanta Zilbra (star)|Thanta Zilbra]]. The ''Naritus'' was the flagship of an evacuation force sent to the [[Thanta Zilbra system/Legends|Thanta Zilbra system]] in response, ferrying as many of the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] inhabitants aboard the New Republic warships and [[Transport/Legends|transports]] as possible before the [[destruction of Thanta Zilbra]]<ref name="AaS">''[[Assault at Selonia]]''</ref> by the [[pre-Republic era]] [[Centerpoint Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], activated by the crisis' masterminds, the [[Sacorrian Triad]].<ref name="NEC" />

The MC80A cruiser remained in use by the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Fleet]] by the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], during which one MC80A caught a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yorik-ta|escape pod]] carrying the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] in a tractor beam over the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] of the [[Mon Calamari system/Legends|Calamari system]].<ref name="DW" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:July_1983_CINEFEX_wingless.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The filming of a Mon Calamari ship during production of ''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'', prior to the attachment of wings]]
The wingless Mon Calamari starship first appeared, albeit unidentified, in the [[1983]] [[Star Wars saga|''Star Wars'' saga]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="Episode VI" /> While working on the film, [[Industrial Light & Magic]] model artists wanted to pad out the Rebel fleet, which was achieved by modifying their original wingless model into the ''Liberty'' type by adding wings and replacing one engine with another pod.<ref name="SW:C">''[[Star Wars Chronicles]]''</ref>

The name "MC80a Star Cruiser" first appeared in [[West End Games]]' [[1990]] ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'', in reference to a Mon Calamari Star Cruiser with undersized wings and dorsal thrusters<ref name="RASB">''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]''</ref> that otherwise resembles the ''Liberty'' type model, previously identified as an MC80 Star Cruiser by ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'' in [[1987]].<ref name="SWSB">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref> Subsequent publications such as the ''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'',<ref name="HttES">''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'',<ref name="XW:TOSG">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> and ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' used the terms "MC80" and "MC80a" interchangeably,<ref name="TTSB">''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> while the [[1994]] [[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|second edition of ''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'']] replaced its reference to the MC80a with the MC80.<ref name="RASB2">[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref>

[[File:XW_MC80a.jpg|thumb|left|300px|A mislabeled MC80 ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser]]
The [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] and its [[1999]] sequel ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' feature Mon Calamari cruisers identified in-game as MC80a models, but visually modeled on the ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser<ref name="XWA">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''</ref> seen in ''Return of the Jedi''.<ref name="Episode VI" /> The intermediate [[X-Wing (game series)|''X-Wing'' game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'' applied the MC80a identifier to another model of Star Cruiser,<ref name="XvT">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]''</ref> also featured in the preceding [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']],<ref name="TF">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref> resembling the illustration of the winged model used for the MC80 and MC80a in West End Games' sourcebooks.<ref name="HttES" />

[[Del Rey]]'s [[1996]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'', written by [[Bill Smith]], redefined the MC80a as a distinct, more powerful variant of the standard MC80 Star Cruiser.<ref name="EGVV" /> This differentiation was repeated by the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 12|twelfth issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'',<ref name="FF12" /> and was elaborated on in the [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'',<ref name="EGW" /> in the endnotes of which co-author [[Paul R. Urquhart]] established that the MC80a corresponded to the "wingless" cruisers seen in ''Return of the Jedi''.<ref name="endnotes12">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/25364458982/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-12|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 12}}</ref>
